name: Architecture College Platform
description:
Merged metadata descriptions from all Architecture College Platform microservices.

# account-service
Handles signup for students, teachers, and admins, JWT login, and authenticated profile access.

# curriculum-service
Academic catalog: careers, areas, professional lines, obligatory and elective subjects, and subject-series relationships.

# enrollment-service
Group and student enrollments, enrollment lock rules, and Mongo-backed bulk preload jobs alongside Postgres transactions.

# grade-service
Captures grades, validation/authorization flows, teacher grading consoles, and academic history projections.

# schedule-service
Class groups (obligatory/elective): create, query, attach teachers, adjust capacity, and emit scheduling-related integration events over RabbitMQ.

# student-service
Student master data: CRUD, lookups, professional line assignment, semester progression, and RabbitMQ integration for domain events.

# teacher-service
Faculty lifecycle: create and delete teachers, validate account numbers, batch lookups, and RabbitMQ integration. JWT validation aligned with account-service.

name: Bank API
description: Minimalist modular-monolith banking REST API — IAM, double-entry accounts, transfers with idempotency, loans, append-only audit, and async notifications. Deployed on AWS EC2 with RDS PostgreSQL, Upstash Redis, and Kafka consumed from a cloud broker; observability stack (Prometheus, Grafana, Loki) runs on EC2.

name: Cinema Platform
description:
Merged metadata descriptions from all Cinema Platform services.

# billboard-service
Enterprise-grade showtime and seat reservation microservice for the Cinema Platform.
Handles scheduling lifecycle, business-rule validation, catalog integration via gRPC,
Redis caching, and optional Kafka event publishing.

# catalog-service
Enterprise-grade catalog microservice for cinemas, theaters, movies, and seats.
Exposes REST and gRPC APIs with Clean Architecture, optional Kafka events,
Redis caching, and JWT role-based access control.

# concession-service
Cinema concession microservice for products, categories, combo meals, and promotional
campaigns. REST API v2 for clients; gRPC ConcessionCatalogService for peer services.
DDD/CQRS with Redis caching, JWT RBAC, and Docker-orchestrated deployment.

# employee-service
HR and workforce microservice for the Cinema Platform. Manages employee records,
work schedules, and vacation requests with JWT role-based access, Redis caching,
and async PostgreSQL persistence. Deployed on AWS EC2 with Amazon RDS and Upstash Redis.

# notification-service
Centralized multi-channel notification microservice for the Cinema Platform.
Delivers Email (SMTP/Jinja2), SMS (Twilio), Push, and In-App notifications
via REST API and Kafka event consumption with MongoDB history and deduplication.

# payment-service
Cinema platform payment microservice — tickets, concessions, merchandise,
subscriptions, and wallet credits. Stripe gateway, PostgreSQL persistence,
Kafka events, role-split REST APIs (customer/staff/admin), and payment method catalog.

# ticket-service
Cinema ticket microservice — purchase, cancel, and use tickets; seat availability
and quotes from Mongo billboard replica; PostgreSQL ticket store; Kafka sync;
outbound gRPC to payment and billboard services.

# user-service
Cinema platform user microservice — registration, JWT sessions, 2FA (TOTP),
profiles, admin user management, Redis session store, Kafka events, and gRPC
directory lookups for other services.

# wallet-service
Digital wallet microservice for the Cinema Platform. Manages customer balances,
credit top-ups, ticket/concession payments, transaction history, and staff
reporting. Deployed on AWS EC2 with Amazon RDS and Upstash Redis; optional
Kafka event publishing and gRPC placeholder for future inter-service RPC.

name: Company Expenses API
description: REST API for corporate expense reporting: employees submit claims with receipts, managers approve or reject, finance processes reimbursements, and admins configure company settings—with JWT security, Flyway migrations, and Docker deploy.

name: Cooking Recipes API
description: REST API for a cooking-recipes platform: user accounts with JWT sessions, recipe CRUD, advanced search, favorites, reviews, and view analytics—built with Clean Architecture and deployable on AWS.

name: Marketing Company API
description: Minimalist GraphQL backend for CRM and marketing operations. Hexagonal architecture on Java 23 and Spring Boot, deployed on AWS (EC2, RDS, Upstash Redis) with external Kafka consumption and Prometheus-ready observability.
techStack: Redis (Upstash in production, local/Docker for dev)

name: MindCare Hub API
description: REST API for small mental-health practices: patient onboarding, specialist scheduling, confidential medical records, billing, and notifications for psychologists, psychiatrists, and therapists.

name: Pimienta Alimentos Backend
description: Production Spring Boot API for Pimienta Alimentos — a real-world company operations platform used daily by employees and staff. Covers HR, payroll, inventory, CRM, contracts, tasks, and file management.

name: Restaurant Management API
description: Production REST API for restaurant operations — JWT auth, menu and stock, table seating, orders, payments with 16% VAT, and reservations. Deployed with AWS RDS PostgreSQL and Upstash Redis (via REDIS_URL).

name: Social Events API
description: REST API for discovering, hosting, and attending social events—organizations, ticket tiers, registrations, comments, in-app notifications, OAuth/JWT auth, and Celery-backed async work. Deployed on AWS EC2 with RDS PostgreSQL and Upstash Redis.

name: Tourist Places API
description: REST API for discovering and managing tourist destinations: countries, categories, places, activities, reviews, and user-curated place lists—with JWT auth, Google OAuth2, and PostgreSQL on AWS RDS.

name: Veterinary Clinic API
description: REST API for veterinary clinic operations—customer and pet onboarding, staff scheduling, clinical sessions (vaccinations, prescriptions, surgeries), payments, and multi-channel notifications. Built with hexagonal architecture, PostgreSQL (sqlc), and Redis-backed JWT sessions.
